copy.title: Giants edge Brewers in shootout. Knicks host Spurs tonight. Monaco Grand Prix this weekend.
copy.keyTakeaway: Giants beat Brewers 12–9, Royals edge Twins 8–6, Cubs top Athletics 7–6. NBA Finals Game 2 tonight (NY @ SA). SPY flat, QQQ down 0.5%. Vampire Lestat and World Cup hype building.

todaysHits.sports: Three close finishes Friday. Finals Game 2 tonight.
todaysHits.worldcup: World Cup 2026 opens June 11 — six days out.
todaysHits.culture: Vampire Lestat turns it up. Doja calls out Elon.

lead.headline: Giants blast Brewers in high-scoring road win
lead.whatHappened: San Francisco put up 12 runs to beat Milwaukee 9 in a slugfest that had both offenses firing. The Giants' bats stayed hot on the road and closed out a tight contest late.
lead.whyBullet1: The Giants are proving they can win ugly games away from home, which matters more than clean victories when you're chasing in a division.
lead.whyBullet2: Milwaukee's inability to hold leads in high-scoring games is becoming a pattern worth watching as the season stretches on.
lead.whatToSay: The Giants looked like the hungrier team yesterday—they just kept answering every punch the Brewers threw.

sportsOther: Giants held on in a slugfest. Brewers couldn't complete the comeback.
Royals' bats stayed hot against Minnesota's pitching.
Cubs edged Oakland in a tight one.

markets.mood: SPY eked out a 0.4% gain while Nvidia jumped 1.8%, but the Nasdaq dropped 0.5% as Tesla fell 1.2% — tech's strength today was selective and narrow.
markets.whyBullet1: The split personality in big tech matters because it shows investors aren't buying the entire sector equally right now. Nvidia's AI chip dominance still draws money, but Tesla's weakness suggests growth expectations are tightening elsewhere.
markets.whyBullet2: Treasury yields climbed 0.5% for the week and 10Y rates moved another -0.3% today — traders are watching Monday's jobs report to see if the Fed stays patient or signals rate changes ahead.
markets.bringUp: The 10-year is up half a percent on the week while the broad market barely moved. That divergence tells you bond traders and stock traders are reading the economy completely differently right now.

golf.headline: Spaun, Clark, Fleetwood tied atop Memorial's brutalist course
golf.whyCare1: Muirfield Village is one of golf's toughest tests and a proving ground before majors kick into high gear.
golf.whyCare2: This field includes most of the guys who'll contend at the Open in a few weeks, so form here matters.
golf.watchFor: Whether Spaun can keep his lead — he's not usually a front-runner in major events, so watch if he folds or holds.
golf.whatToSay: Muirfield's playing so hard this year that even at five under, these guys barely feel safe.

copy.finalSharpTake: Tech stocks are split right down the middle today—NVDA grinding higher while TSLA stumbles—which tells you nobody agrees on where growth goes next. The S&P barely moved, the Nasdaq dropped, and that's not indecision, that's caution. Meanwhile the Giants just beat the Brewers 12–9 in a slugfest, the kind of game that reminds you baseball season's heating up fast. NBA Finals Game 2 tips tonight. Monaco is this weekend. World Cup opens in six days. A lot of noise coming.
